title: Mirror column viewable for guests

posts.author: Isa1bella1 (Bella M )
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

As an agency we use monday.com a lot for client communication but also for the clients to see scheduled work.

With multiple clients, we use an internal hub to schedule all our work and ticket in here are connected to the belonging ticket in our clients individual boards.

It would be amazing if we could mirror columns from the internal hub into the client boards without giving the clients access to the internal board.

This would mean the client could see when a task has been scheduled for and how many hrs have been scheduled, allowing transparency and it would mean we the client team don’t have to update 2 places.

Thanks!

What are you trying to achieve

A unrestricted mirror columns so guest can see data from another board without having access to the entire board. But only see data we allow them to see, such as which date a task has been scheduled for.

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

If may suggest a workaround for what you trying to achieve, you can use SuperForm which allows you to share specific item data(including mirror columns) with people outside of Monday.

SuperForm - monday.com Marketplace SuperForm - monday.com Marketplace

Update Existing Items in monday.com https://share.google/GbQBqnDiw825Kv84r

posts.author: dmitry_noliam (Dmitry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Isa1bella1 ,

I guess the guests don’t have access to the Internal Hub → thus not able to see any mirrored values as they can’t see what they are not supposed to access.

There are two ideas that might work for you:

  • You can create a view with only the fields visible and then share it as a view (bear in mind that the link shared is accessible for everyone with the link - not tied to monday account users)
  • The second option is a little trickier and depends a little on your setup: You can make the internal hub shareable and invite guests directly to this board and edit the permissions on the columns and items to not show everything to the guests but only what they are supposed to see.

Option 3 could also be a little automation in make.com that automatically updates values with given triggers.
